title: Optometry Practitioners Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct Codes, 2011
title_en: Optometry Practitioners Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct Codes, 2011

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Codes may be cited as Optometry Practitioners Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct Codes, 2011 and shall come into operation on the date of publication.

provisions.title: Principal
provisions.content: The principles for Ethics and Professional Conduct for an Optometry Practitioners shall be— (a) confidentiality and privacy; (b) accountability; (c) self confidence; (d) competence and professional advancement; (e) truthfulness, fidelity and loyalty; (f) safety and protection; (g) justice; (h) good relationship; and (i) personal presentation and attire.

provisions.title: Obligation of the optometry practitioner
provisions.content: (1) An Optometry practitioner shall be responsible for the overall management of a client’s requests, investigations, examinations, production and treatment of timely findings or results. (2) The obligation to avoid doing harm is a human value which guides a Optometry Practitioner to act positively to safeguard and promote the interest of the individual, client and society in general. (3) An Optometry Practitioners shall, in avoiding doing harm— (a) be constantly on guard in judgment in the face of many outside pressures that may be exerted on him; (b) be entitled to decline requests to conduct optometry investigations and treatment, which he believes to be inappropriate or detrimental to the client’s welfare; (c) refuse to condone, facilitate, allow or coordinate optometry investigation and treatment which are detrimental to the client’s welfare; (d) practice his profession with conscience and dignity; (e) not delegate to a person who is not a qualified optometry practitioner tasks of optometry works; and (f) take appropriate action if the workload and pressure on professional colleagues and juniors may endanger safe standards of practice.

provisions.title: Confidentiality and privacy
provisions.content: (1) An Optometry' Practitioners shall— (a) respect the confidential and personal nature of professional records; (b) protect the patient’s right to privacy by keeping the information in the strictest confidence; (c) be aware that the information given by a patient to an Optometry Practitioner remains the property of the patient; and (d) be aware that the findings, which an Optometry Practitioner obtain as a result of investigation and treatment, remains to be confidential. (2) An optometry practitioner shall not disclose the patient’s information unless— (a) a patient given consent to disclosure; (b) the clinician or other authorized health care professional ordered optometry investigation and treatment on patient request the results; (c) information is required by the due legal processes; or (d) information required, is to be shared with other Optometry Practitioners or other members of health professions given in the official clinical reports.

provisions.title: Accountability
provisions.content: (1) An Optometry Practitioner shall be accountable for any action which contradicts the provisions of these Regulations and he shall— (a) comply with provisions of the Act and other laws regulating the profession; (b) observe professional responsibility for practice and adhere to standards of practice and total quality system; (c) only accept requests for optometry investigation and treatment which are properly authorized in accordance with established or recognized criteria; (d) be accountable for his work; (e) in the event that an accident is occasioned to a client in the course of management, inform the relevant authority measures taken or to be undertaken; (f) handle the client and any person accompanying him with due courtesy and respect; (g) avoid interpersonal relationships that can impair professional judgment and proper assessment of risks; (h) be responsible for the logical process from the acquisition of the requests to the production of data and the final report of the results; and (i) be accoutable for the quality and integrity of optometry clinical services.

provisions.title: Self confidence
provisions.content: (1) An Optometry Practitioner shall to build and develop positive and realistic attitude, trust his personal ability and possess sense of control of decisions or judgment. (2) An optometry practitioner shall, in developing and building positive and realistic attitude— (a) uphold and maintain the dignity and respect of the profession and strive for maintaining a reputation of honesty, integrity and reliability; and (b) hold himself out as a person who, by training and experience, is professionally qualified to diagnose and treat diseases.

provisions.title: Competence
provisions.content: (1) An Optometry Pratitioners shall— (a) perform his work within the scope of his competence and professional preparation; (b) take every reasonable opportunity to improve and sustain his knowledge, skills and professional competence; (c) request for support when he perceives the need; (d) provide expert advice and consult other health professionals; (e) be dedicated to the use of optometry sciences to benefit mankind; (f) exercise professional judgment, skills and care while meeting established standards and quality systems; (g) strive for improvement of professional skills and knowledge and adopt scientific advancement that benefits the patient and other users; and (h) strive for improvement of the delivery of reliable investigations and treatments results.

provisions.title: Truthfulness, fidelity and loyalty
provisions.content: (1) An Optometry shall, when performing his duties be honest and truthful. (2) An Optometry Practitioner shall respect the inherent trust that exists in his relationship with the client; to communicate truthfully and without deception or deceit. (3) An Optometry Practitioner shall, where there is an adverse event to a procedure, drug or device, communicate that information to relevant authorities. (4) An Optometry Practitioner shall observe fidelity, keep promises made and not represent himself in a false or misleading manner. (5) An Optometry Practitioner shall not perform or recommend optometry investigation or procedures that are not necessary or share fees for providing optometry services in a false or misleading manner.

provisions.title: Justice
provisions.content: An Optometry Practitioner shall— (a) use wisdom and judgment to ensure that the limited resources are used fairly; and (b) treat all clients fairly and equitably based on needs, regardless of the client’s economic status, religion, race, sex, tribe, age, political affiliation, physical or any other similar attributes.

provisions.title: Relationships
provisions.content: (1) An Optometry Practitioner shall, for the purposes of strengthening relationship with clients and patients— (a) inform a person with whom he has a professional contact, the nature of the relationship; (b) respect the customs, value, spiritual beliefs and human dignity of patients; (c) respect the physical and physiological needs of patients; (d) avoid any abuse of the privilege relationship with patients; (e) promote and safeguard the well being and interests of patients; (f) refuse to accept any gift, favour or hospitality that might be interpreted as seeking to exegete under influence to obtain preferential treatment; and (g) take into consideration the fact that clients, patients and accompanying relatives can easily be embarrassed, ashamed or fearful in the course of collecting requests for optometry investigations; (h) show humanity, kindness and understanding towards them; (i) do his best to relieve the client, patient or their relative of hidden fears and apprehension; (j) encourage and restore confidence in the client with regard to consent to the optometry investigation and treatment. (2) An optometry practitioner shall, before carrying out optometry investigation and treatment against a patient, obtain the patient or patient relative consent. (3) The consent obtain under sub-codes (2) shall not be misused by an optometry practitioner and it shall be valid when given freely and readily where the client understand the nature, purpose and consequences of what is proposed. (4) Every Optometry Practitioner shall cooperate professionally with his colleagues and other health related professionals and he shall— (a) regard colleagues as brothers and sisters and respect each other; (b) share knowledge, skills and experiences with colleagues; (c) avoid any action, which may be regarded as self-praise; condemning or belittling colleagues or using derogatory language about them; and; (d) recognize and respect the expertise and contribution of other workers and collaborate with them to provide the best optometry services and care.

provisions.title: Personal presentation and attire
provisions.content: An Optometry Practitioner shall, at place of work— (a) appear smart, properly and decently dressed in proper attire with identification tags and behave in a manner becoming of professional; (b) be at all times courteous to patients and clients, and be considerate to colleagues; and (c) not consume alcohol or take drugs that could interfere with his performance during working hours or when on emergency stand-by.

provisions.title: Research and consultancy
provisions.content: (1) An Optometry Practitioner shall— (a) ensure protection of the welfare and rights of subjects used in research; (b) ensure that research is conducted after approval by an approved Ethical Review Committee and only qualified persons are allowed to conduct a research; (c) preserve dignity and privacy of research subjects at all times; (d) ensure that consent to do research is obtained and observe the rights of the subject to withdraw consent; and (e) be aware of current advancements in science and technology, including genetic engineering.

provisions.title: HIV/AIDS
provisions.content: (1) An Optometry Practitioner shall, when dealing with optimistic diseases and diagnosis of HIV/AIDS— (a) observe confidentiality in saving human life against deliberate acts likely to infect potential victims; (b) acquire adequate knowledge of HIV/AIDS transmission, prevention and skills on counseling; (c) educate individuals infected with HIV/AIDS on the opportunity to agree or decline from being research subjects and on the methods applied for scientific research and teaching, including taking of photographs; and (d) have a duty to take appropriate precautions to protect their patients and staff from cross-infection.

provisions.title: Disaster and emergency situations
provisions.content: (1) An Optometry Practitioner shall practice his profession with conscience and dignity and the health of the clients shall be his first consideration. (2) An Optometry Practitioner shall be obliged during the threatening emergencies to take immediate steps to ensure that necessary radiological services are performed to the patients without discrimination or undue delay.

provisions.title: Publicity, advertisement and canvassing
provisions.content: (1) An Optometry Practitioner shall maintain good relationship with his colleagues for the purposes of fostering teamwork in Optometry Profession and be aware that matters of general interest are sacred and refrain from adopting methods aimed at advertising a particular person institutions, examinations, treatment or technique. (2) An Optometry Practitioner shall not— (a) advertise and sign using professional qualifications to encourage the sale of commercial products; (b) use any professional premises to display the name of commercial product; (c) publish any article or personal photograph or otherwise indulge in any form of self-advertisement or publicity; (d) encourage any practice, which is of a nature that invites attention to professional position, skill and qualification of achievements; (e) publish or cause to be published in a lay media articles likely to publicize, advertise and canvass; (f) communicate or address to the lay public, use or permit the use of personal professional qualification as an advertisement for the organization or the company or be personally involved in advertising company services; (g) talk in a derogatory manner about the professional skills, knowledge, qualification or services of another practitioner, or (h) canvas for purposes of obtaining, whether directly or through an agent associated with or employed by an organization or a company.

provisions.title: Conflict of interest
provisions.content: An Optometry Practitioner shall abide to regulations and avoid situations, which may create conflicts of interest including compliance with the Public Procurement Act in obtaining supplies.

provisions.title: Oath
provisions.content: (1) An optometry practitioner shall take oath to mark the fulfillment of profession requirements before the Chancellor of the relevant University or relevant recognized legal authority of the training institution. Schedule (Made under regulation 17(1)) The pledge The Oath shall be as follows— I ____________________ solemnly swear/affirm in the presence of this assembly, that I will fulfill my duties with loyalty and I will end eavour to maintain and elevate the standards of my profession. I will hold in confidence and confidentiality all information availed to me during the practice of the profession. In the course of my practice, I will not accept or give bribery in exchange for service, perform any tests contrary to established procedures, give false results or do any other action which will endanger the health of clients, my colleagues, public and the safety of the environment.
